text.title: Risk Assessment Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Risk Assessment methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Likelihood MUST be expressed as P10/P50/P90 numeric probabilities; vague 'low/medium/high' values are rejected.
rule.rationale: Verbal likelihood drifts in meaning; numeric bands force calibration.

rule.statement: Every risk MUST name an individual owner; 'team' or 'TBD' is not an owner.
rule.rationale: Unowned risks rot; specific owners create accountability.

rule.statement: Every risk MUST carry an observable trigger phrased as 'metric X crosses threshold Y' (not vague prose).
rule.rationale: Triggers translate the risk into a runtime monitor.

rule.statement: Every risk MUST have a decision: escalate | accept | mitigate; no 'monitor' (that is the trigger, not the decision).
Risk register MUST cap at top 7 risks; lower-ranked risks land in parked-risks.md.
rule.rationale: Beyond 7 risks the register becomes a wishlist nobody acts on.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
